title: Gazette Notification (APCTT)
text: REGD. NO. D.L.-33004/94 The Gazette Of India EXTRAORDINARY PART II--Section 3—Sub-section (ii) PUBLISHED BY AUTHORITY No. 40] NEW DELHI, TUESDAY, JANUARY 17,1935/PAUSA 27, 1916 139 GI/95 (1) -- 1 of 4 -- 2 THfi GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART 11-SBC 3(Ji)] MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, jahe 9th January, 1995 S.O. 46(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by section 3 of the United Nations (Privileges and Immunities) Act, 1947 (46 of 1947) the Central Government hereby declares that the provisions of Article I (Section 1). Article II (Sections 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 & S), Article III (Section 9 & 10), Article IV (Section 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 and 16), Article V (Sections 17, 18, 20, and 21), Article VI (Sections 22 and 23), Article VII (Sections 24, 25 and 26) and Article VIII (Section 29) of the Schedule to the said Act shall apply mutatis mutandis to the Asian and Pacific Centre for Transfer of Technology and is representatives and officials. 2. This notification shall be deemed to have come into force on the 7th day of April, 1994. [F. No. UI|252|15|93] SAVITRI KUNADI, Jt. Secy. -- 2 of 4 -- 3 EXPLANATORY MEMORANDUM The Government of India have, on the 7th April, 1994 entered into an agreement to accord to (lie Asian and Pacific Centre for Transfer of Technology, its representatives and officials, the same privileges and immuni- ties as are accorded to ,the United Nations Offices in India, their representatives and officials. The Headquarters of the Asian and Pacific Centre for Transfer of Technology in New Delhi was opened in December, 1993. Accordingly, it is necessary for this notification to be made operative retrospectively from 7th April J994. By giving retrospective operation to this notification, no one's interests will be prejudicially affected. Printed by the Manager, Govl. of India Press, Ring Road, Maya Puri, New DclhHJ0064 and Publijhcd by the Controller of Publications, Delhi-110054, 1995 -- 3 of 4 -- -- 4 of 4 --
